description:
## Background

`IssueExplorer.tsx` is carrying more than rendering concerns. As the issue explorer grows, it has accumulated UI state coordination and derived view-model logic alongside JSX:

- resolving the active Issue List View
- composing base vs searched visible rows
- deriving list empty-state signals
- coordinating selected issue lookup
- passing derived values through list/detail panes

Some of this belongs near the React shell, but much of it is issue-explorer model logic that would be easier to test and evolve from a focused non-React module. This is a side-quest refactor to do after the filtering/search epic work settles, not part of that epic.

This ticket also absorbs the narrower cleanup previously captured in `bsm-dbh.12`: `IssueListEmptyReason` and its derivation should not live in `IssueExplorer.tsx` long-term. That type originates from issue-list derived state, not from the rendering component.

## What to build

Refactor `src/issues/IssueExplorer.tsx` so it is primarily a rendering and event-wiring component. Move reusable/derivable issue-explorer logic into a focused `lib`/model file, for example one of:

- `src/issues/issue-explorer-state.ts`
- `src/issues/issue-explorer-model.ts`
- `src/issues/lib/issue-explorer-state.ts`

The exact path can be chosen during implementation, but the intent is a non-React module that owns issue explorer derived state.

Likely candidates to extract:

- active view normalization/defaulting
- base collection + searched visible collection composition
- selected issue lookup from the visible collection
- empty/list state derivation that is not purely presentational, including `IssueListEmptyReason`
- any small derived booleans or values that currently require multi-line logic in the component

Keep React-only concerns in the component, such as `useState`, event handlers, DOM refs, JSX, and accessibility attributes.

## Constraints

- This is a behavior-preserving refactor.
- Do not change user-facing copy or visual layout.
- Do not add new product behavior unless a separate issue asks for it.
- Avoid creating a vague grab-bag utility file. The extracted module should have a clear domain name and cohesive API.
- Prefer direct unit tests for extracted logic over expanding DOM-heavy component tests.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] `IssueExplorer.tsx` has less inline derived-state logic and is mostly rendering/event wiring.
- [ ] `IssueListEmptyReason` is no longer defined in `IssueExplorer.tsx`.
- [ ] Extracted issue-explorer logic lives in a focused non-React module with a cohesive API.
- [ ] The extracted module has direct unit tests for important derived-state cases, including base-empty vs search-filtered-empty.
- [ ] Existing Issue Explorer behavior remains unchanged.
- [ ] Existing component tests continue to pass.
- [ ] No new product behavior, copy changes, or layout changes are introduced.

## Test Plan

- Add unit tests for the extracted state/model module.
- Run `pnpm test`.
- Run `pnpm check`.
- Run `pnpm exec tsc --noEmit`.
title: Refactor IssueExplorer UI logic into a model module
